title: Usury and Interest
subtitle: A Declaration of War Against Allah and His Messenger

verses.arabic: الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ الرِّبَا لَا يَقُومُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَقُومُ الَّذِي يَتَخَبَّطُهُ الشَّيْطَانُ مِنَ الْمَسِّ
verses.translation: Those who consume interest cannot stand [on the Day of Resurrection] except as one stands who is being beaten by Satan into insanity.

verses.arabic: يَمْحَقُ اللَّهُ الرِّبَا وَيُرْبِي الصَّدَقَاتِ ۗ وَاللَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ كَفَّارٍ أَثِيمٍ
verses.translation: Allah destroys interest and gives increase for charities. And Allah does not like every sinning disbeliever.

verses.arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا لَا تَأْكُلُوا الرِّبَا أَضْعَافًا مُّضَاعَفَةً ۖ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
verses.translation: O you who have believed, do not consume usury, doubled and multiplied, but fear Allah that you may be successful.

brief_explanation: The Quran describes those who consume riba (interest) as rising on the Day of Resurrection in a state of madness — seized and convulsed as if possessed by a devil — a vivid depiction of spiritual ruin earned in this world through greed. Allah declares that He 'destroys riba' in this world and the next, stripping the blessing (barakah) from wealth built on exploitation. Most strikingly, Allah issued the only direct declaration of war in the Quran against those who persist in dealing with riba after being warned: 'If you do not [give it up], then be informed of a war [against you] from Allah and His Messenger' (2:279). This makes riba among the most explicitly and severely condemned economic sins in the Book of Allah.
capital_punishment: There is no hadd (fixed corporal punishment) prescribed for riba. It is a major sin that requires stopping the prohibited dealings, returning any excess taken, and making sincere repentance. The Islamic state has authority to enforce prohibition of riba through regulation and ta'zir (discretionary fines, imprisonment, or other measures). Scholars note that if riba involves fraud or coercion causing material harm, additional criminal penalties may apply under the general principles of Islamic justice.
